turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough for about 5 days now. It started dry and then became productive with yellowish-green sputum, which is really concerning me because it doesn't seem like your typical cold symptoms.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: Yeah, it's been keeping me up at night and making it hard to focus at work. I've tried taking dextromethorphan for a week now, but it only seems to give me minimal relief - it doesn't really stop the coughing.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I've also had some difficulty breathing and wheezing when I'm trying to sleep or even just lying down. And sometimes, especially in the evenings, I'll get a low-grade fever that's around 38.2°C (100.8°F).

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, yes - last night was particularly bad, and I woke up with my pajamas completely soaked through from night sweats. That's happened for 4 nights now, which is really worrying me.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The coughing and wheezing seem to be mainly in my chest area, but sometimes it feels like it's spreading up into my throat when I'm trying to talk or swallow. And I've also been feeling this fatigue and malaise that just seems to linger everywhere, you know?

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: Honestly, it's not just my physical health that's worrying me - I'm also feeling really anxious about maybe having something contagious since I work with kids and they're always bringing home illnesses from school. And to be honest, my allergies haven't been acting up too badly lately, which is unusual for this time of year.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: I've actually had some swelling in my lymph nodes under my ears and in my neck, but it's been pretty mild so far - I wouldn't say it's anything too out of the ordinary for when I have a cold or allergies.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: No, I haven't really experienced any nausea or stomach issues so far, but I've been a bit concerned about it because several kids in my class at school were recently out sick with upper respiratory infections. That's making me worry even more about possibly having something contagious that could spread to them.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: To be honest, my energy levels have been really low lately - I've missed three days of work already because I just didn't feel up to it. And as for my appetite, I'd say it's probably decreased a bit too, although I'm not sure if that's directly related to the cough or just my overall fatigue and anxiety about everything going on with my body right now.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've actually been experiencing low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's worse in the evenings, and night sweats, which have soaked through my pajamas every night for 4 nights now. It's really concerning me because I don't want to risk getting anyone else sick, especially since I work with young children daily.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I have seasonal allergies, allergic rhinitis, which has been going on since I was a teenager. I take cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season to manage my symptoms. Other than that, everything's been pretty normal from what I remember from my last physical exam about a year ago.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've actually been trying to take my cetirizine 10mg as needed for my allergies, but I haven't really needed it much lately since they're not acting up too badly. And as for dextromethorphan, like I mentioned earlier, I've taken it for a week now with minimal relief, so I'm not sure if I should keep trying to stick with that or look into something else.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, my eating habits have been pretty irregular lately due to my fatigue and lack of appetite. I usually try to stick to simple foods like soups or toast when I'm feeling down, but nothing really sounds appealing most days. My partner has actually been cooking for me a lot more often because I just don't have the energy to prepare meals myself.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't been able to find the motivation to work out or do much physical activity lately due to my fatigue and just feeling miserable with this cough. It's hard for me to even make it through a day without feeling exhausted, so exercise has definitely taken a backseat right now.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mother actually has asthma. She's been managing it with medication and inhalers for years, so that's something I've grown up knowing about, but I'm not sure if there's any connection to what I'm experiencing now, especially since her symptoms are more related to respiratory issues triggered by allergens or stress.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, I do worry about my own health because of it. To be honest, every time I think back to my mom's asthma and how it affects her, it makes me anxious about the possibility that I might have something similar going on with this persistent cough and difficulty breathing. It's hard not to make connections when family history is involved.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I do have a moderate amount of social alcohol use - usually 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends when my partner and I are relaxing together. As for coping with stress, to be honest, it's been really tough lately because this cough has been making me feel so anxious and worried about what might be going on with my health. I've tried taking a few extra deep breaths, practicing some relaxation techniques, and talking to my partner when they're home from work, but it's hard not to get caught up in worst-case scenarios when you're dealing with something as unpredictable as this persistent cough.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke and I've never smoked. As for substances, other than taking cetirizine 10mg as needed for my allergies and dextromethorphan for this cough, I'm not using anything else that I can think of.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I appreciate your reassurance. Honestly, I have been a bit anxious about sharing some things because I'm worried about being judged or thinking that it might be unrelated to my symptoms. But if I'm being completely honest, I do have one concern that's been weighing on me - my allergic reaction to penicillin caused a rash when I was younger, and while it wasn't severe, it made me really nervous about taking any antibiotics in the future. However, since this cough has persisted and I've had these other symptoms like fever and night sweats, I'm worried that I might need some kind of antibiotic treatment, but I don't know if that would be safe for me to take.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I guess I've been thinking a lot about my job and being around kids all day. As an elementary school teacher, I'm always trying to make sure I don't get anyone sick, but with this cough and fever, it's making me really anxious about potentially spreading something to the kids or even just being contagious in general.
